report_name: HIBP Adds 56 Million Emails from Massive Infostealer Log Compilation

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: Infostealer malware is distributed through methods requiring user interaction, such as opening malicious email attachments, or running cracked software or deceptive downloads from sources like YouTube or Discord.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: Information-stealing malware programmatically accesses and extracts saved usernames and passwords from the local storage of web browsers, email clients, and FTP clients.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: Infostealer malware collects files from the victim's desktop or document folders as part of its data harvesting routine.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
attack_patterns.description: Information-stealing malware harvests session cookies from web browsers to facilitate account takeover attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: A log file containing stolen credentials, cookies, and system information is compressed and sent to the attacker's command-and-control server.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: A command-line pattern involving 'powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File' was noted as a potential indicator of malicious script execution associated with infostealer activity.

malware.name: Information-Stealing Malware
malware.description: A general category of malware designed to harvest sensitive information from a victim's computer, such as credentials, cookies, and files. This entry represents the various stealers contributing to the 'June 2026 Stealer Logs' dataset.

identities.name: Have I Been Pwned
identities.description: A data breach notification service that incorporated the 'June 2026 Stealer Logs' dataset, containing 56.3 million unique email addresses, to allow users to check for compromises.

infrastructure.name: Attacker Command and Control Server
infrastructure.description: C2 servers used by various infostealer campaigns to receive exfiltrated log files containing stolen data.

infrastructure.name: Exfiltration Destination Platform
infrastructure.description: Public platforms such as Telegram or Pastebin used as destinations for exfiltrated data from infostealer malware.

indicators.name: Potential Infostealer Exfiltration to Public Services
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Potential Infostealer Exfiltration to Public Services
id: 7f5b1a3d-8c4e-4f9a-9b1d-0c8a7b6e5d4c
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to public services like Pastebin or Telegram, which are sometimes used by infostealers for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://haveibeenpwned.com/Breach/June2026StealerLogs
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1041
level: medium
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate user traffic to these services.
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationHostname|endswith:
      - 'pastebin.com'
      - 'telegram.org'
    Initiated: true
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects outbound network connections to public services like Pastebin and Telegram, which can be used by infostealer malware for data exfiltration. The confidence is medium as these are also legitimate services.

indicators.name: Access to Chrome Login Data File
indicators.pattern:
title: Access to Chrome Login Data File by Non-Browser Process
id: 1e9d0f8c-7b6a-4d5e-8f6g-1h2i3j4k5l6m
status: experimental
description: Detects processes other than Google Chrome accessing the file that stores saved credentials. This is a strong indicator of credential theft by infostealer malware.
references:
  - https://haveibeenpwned.com/Breach/June2026StealerLogs
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1555.003
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate backup software or other security tools.
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: '\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Login Data'
  filter:
    Image|endswith: '\chrome.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects non-browser processes accessing the Google Chrome 'Login Data' file, a common behavior of infostealer malware harvesting credentials.

indicators.name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass with File
id: 2d8c1e7b-6a5b-4c3d-9e4f-2g3h4i5j6k7l
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of PowerShell with the '-ExecutionPolicy Bypass' and '-File' parameters, a common pattern for executing malicious scripts.
references:
  - https://haveibeenpwned.com/Breach/June2026StealerLogs
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: low
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts.
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '-ExecutionPolicy Bypass'
      - '-File'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a PowerShell command-line pattern that bypasses the execution policy to run a script file, which can be indicative of malicious activity.
